title: neoporteria thiebautiana
intro: Neoporteria thiebautiana, also known as the Chilean Pincushion or Eriosyce thiebautiana, is a species of cactus in the genus Neoporteria. It is native to the Atacama Desert in Chile, and its stems are covered with sharp, short spines. The Neoporteria thiebautiana is believed to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations made from the plant can be used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and digestive problems, and are often consumed in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions.
